heading: Reporting requirements regarding coal or other mine safety
label: 78m–2 Reporting requirements regarding coal or other mine safety

children.label: A
children.text: the total number of violations of mandatory health or safety standards that could significantly and substantially contribute to the cause and effect of a coal or other mine safety or health hazard under section 104 of the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 () for which the operator received a citation from the Mine Safety and Health Administration;30 U.S.C. 814

children.label: B
children.text: the total number of orders issued under section 104(b) of such Act ();30 U.S.C. 814(b)

children.label: C
children.text: the total number of citations and orders for unwarrantable failure of the mine operator to comply with mandatory health or safety standards under section 104(d) of such Act ();30 U.S.C. 814(d)

children.label: D
children.text: the total number of flagrant violations under section 110(b)(2) of such Act ();30 U.S.C. 820(b)(2)

children.label: E
children.text: the total number of imminent danger orders issued under section 107(a) of such Act ();30 U.S.C. 817(a)

children.label: F
children.text: the total dollar value of proposed assessments from the Mine Safety and Health Administration under such Act ( et seq.); and30 U.S.C. 801

children.label: G
children.text: the total number of mining-related fatalities.

children.label: A
children.text: a pattern of violations of mandatory health or safety standards that are of such nature as could have significantly and substantially contributed to the cause and effect of coal or other mine health or safety hazards under section 104(e) of such Act (); or30 U.S.C. 814(e)

children.label: B
children.text: the potential to have such a pattern.
Any pending legal action before the Federal Mine Safety and Health Review Commission involving such coal or other mine.

children.heading: Reporting shutdowns and patterns of violations
children.label: b Reporting shutdowns and patterns of violations

children.text: The receipt of an imminent danger order issued under section 107(a) of the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 ().30 U.S.C. 817(a)

children.label: A
children.text: a pattern of violations of mandatory health or safety standards that are of such nature as could have significantly and substantially contributed to the cause and effect of coal or other mine health or safety hazards under section 104(e) of such Act (); or30 U.S.C. 814(e)

children.label: B
children.text: the potential to have such a pattern.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: c Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to affect any obligation of a person to make a disclosure under any other applicable law in effect before, on, or after .July 21, 2010

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 1 Enforcement
children.text: A violation by any person of this section, or any rule or regulation of the Commission issued under this section, shall be treated for all purposes in the same manner as a violation of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ( et seq.) or the rules and regulations issued thereunder, consistent with the provisions of this section, and any such person shall be subject to the same penalties, and to the same extent, as for a violation of such Act or the rules or regulations issued thereunder.15 U.S.C. 78a

children.heading: Rules and regulations
children.label: 2 Rules and regulations
children.text: The Commission is authorized to issue such rules or regulations as are necessary or appropriate for the protection of investors and to carry out the purposes of this section.
the terms “issuer” and “securities laws” have the meaning given the terms in section 3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ();15 U.S.C. 78c

the term “coal or other mine” means a coal or other mine, as defined in section 3 of the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 (), that is subject to the provisions of such Act ( et seq.); and30 U.S.C. 80230 U.S.C. 801
the term “operator” has the meaning given the term in section 3 of the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 ().30 U.S.C. 802

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: f Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect on the day that is 30 days after .July 21, 2010
